specification.topic-statement: The agent registry maintains the in-memory array of active agents and provides add, remove, and lookup operations over that array.

scope.in-scope: Adding agents to the array with dynamic capacity growth, removing agents by exact UUID, finding agents by exact or prefix UUID match, and checking whether a UUID prefix matches multiple agents.
scope.boundary: This topic stores pointers to agent records and reads their UUID field for lookup. It does not manage agent lifecycle, memory allocation of the agents themselves, or database persistence.
Switching between agents and updating the navigation display context are handled separately. This topic only manages the array contents.

field.name: agent
field.note: null if not found, ambiguous, or prefix too short

behavior.adding-an-agent:
If the current agent count equals the current capacity, the array is resized before insertion: - If capacity is zero, the new capacity is set to 4. - Otherwise, the capacity doubles.
If the resize allocation fails, the process aborts immediately (no error is returned). After ensuring capacity, the agent pointer is appended to the array and the count is incremented. This operation never returns an error.
behavior.removing-an-agent:
The array is scanned linearly using exact string comparison against each agent's UUID. If no match is found, an AGENT_NOT_FOUND error is returned with the requested UUID included in the message.
If a match is found: - If the matched agent is the current agent, the current pointer is set to null. - All agents after the matched index are shifted down by one position (compaction preserving order). - The count is decremented.
If the removed agent was the current agent, the REPL's current agent pointer becomes null after this operation. No automatic switch to another agent occurs. Any subsequent access to the current agent by a caller that does not check for null will dereference a null pointer. Callers are responsible for detecting and resolving this state.
behavior.finding-an-agent-by-uuid-prefix:
If the prefix length is less than 4 characters, null is returned immediately.
Otherwise, a first pass scans the array for an exact match using full string comparison. If an exact match is found, it is returned immediately without proceeding to prefix matching.
If no exact match exists, a second pass scans for prefix matches using a comparison bounded to the prefix length. The number of matches is counted: - If exactly one match is found after the full scan, it is returned. - If a second match is found during scanning, null is returned immediately (ambiguous prefix). - If no matches are found, null is returned.
behavior.checking-prefix-ambiguity:
If the prefix length is less than 4 characters, returns false.
Otherwise, counts all agents whose UUID begins with the prefix. If two or more matches are found, returns true immediately. Returns false if fewer than two agents match.
A prefix shorter than 4 characters returns false (not ambiguous) rather than an error. Callers that rely on ambiguity detection must separately validate that the prefix meets the minimum length before interpreting a false result as "unambiguous."
